title: Ensure 'Python version' is currently supported (if in use)

input_row.title: Ensure 'Python version' is currently supported (if in use)
input_row.description: Periodically, older versions of Python may be deprecated and no longer supported. Using a supported version of Python for function app deployment slots is recommended to avoid potential unpatched vulnerabilities.
input_row.rationale: Deprecated and unsupported versions of programming and scripting languages can present vulnerabilities which may not be addressed or may not be addressable.
input_row.audit: Take note of the currently supported versions (given a status of "security") of Python here: https://devguide.python.org/versions/ Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings pane, ensure that for a Stack of Python, the Python Version reflects a currently supported release. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each function app and deployment slot. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list function apps: az functionapp list For each function app, run the following command to list deployment slots: az functionapp deployment slot list --resource-group -- name For each deployment slot, run the following command to get the Python version: az resource show --name web --resource-group -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type config --parent sites//slots/ --query properties.[linuxFxVersion,pythonVersion] If Python is in use, ensure the version is currently supported. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 829b40f3-d3db-4fd2-be46-76663d3aeeb2 - Name: 'Function app slots that use Python should use a specified 'Python version''
input_row.remediation: Note: No action is required if Python is not in use. Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings pane, for a Stack of Python, set the Python Version to a currently supported release. 7. Click Save. 8. Click Continue. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each function app and deployment slot requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI Run the following command to list supported runtimes: az functionapp list-runtimes For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command with the appropriate parameters to update the Python version: az resource update --name web --resource-group -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type config --parent sites//slots/ --set properties.linuxFxVersion="PYTHON|"
input_row.impact: If your app is written using version-dependent features or libraries, they may not be available on more recent versions. If you wish to update, research the impact thoroughly.
input_row.default_value: The version is selected during creation.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Open the AWS Management Console and navigate to the **Lambda** service.
2. Select **Functions** from the Lambda dashboard to see the list of your deployed Lambda functions.
3. Click on the name of a function that may be using Python.
4. Scroll down to the **Environment variables** configuration or the **Runtime** field in the **Configuration** tab.
5. Check the **Runtime** to see if it is set to a Python version that aligns with the currently supported releases. Refer to the following link: [Python Supported Versions](https://devguide.python.org/versions/).
6. If you have multiple functions utilizing Python, repeat steps 3-5 for each function.

**What to verify/validate:**
- Ensure that the Runtime specified for each function app is a currently supported version of Python.

**Expected secure configuration:**
- Each function should list a Python Runtime that has an active support status as per the referenced website.

**How to confirm compliance:**
- If all function versions used are listed as supported on the provided link, then the compliance requirement is met. If any function is using an unsupported version, it's a compliance failure that should be remediated by updating the function configuration.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** DISAGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The classification as "Manual" is not entirely correct. This compliance check can be automated using AWS APIs, specifically with the AWS SDK for Python (boto3) or AWS CLI. AWS Lambda functions can be programmatically queried to retrieve their runtime configurations, which can then be cross-referenced with the list of supported Python versions.
  
- **AWS SDK Capabilities:** You can use boto3 to list all Lambda functions and check their runtime. The `list_functions()` method in boto3 can be used to retrieve details about each function, including the runtime version. This can be automated to ensure all functions are using a supported Python version.

- **AWS CLI Commands:** The AWS CLI command `aws lambda list-functions` can be used to obtain a list of all Lambda functions and their configurations, including the runtime. This can be scripted to automate the compliance check.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 6

- **Automation Details:** The original assessment lacks automation details. It does not mention the possibility of using boto3 or AWS CLI, which are valid methods for automating this check.
  
- **Manual Steps Accuracy:** The manual steps are generally accurate for a console-based check but are incomplete without mentioning automation possibilities.
  
- **Program Name Convention:** The program name is not provided, but a suitable name following the convention could be `aws_lambda_runtime_python_version_check`.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Automation Suggestion:** Implement an automated script using boto3 to list all Lambda functions and their runtimes. Compare these against the supported Python versions list to ensure compliance.
  
- **Missing Considerations:** The assessment should consider the use of AWS Config rules or AWS Lambda functions to automate the compliance check. AWS Config can be used to continuously monitor the compliance of Lambda functions' runtime versions.
  
- **Alternative AWS API Methods:** Use `boto3.client('lambda').list_functions()` to retrieve function details programmatically.

- **Better Program Name Suggestion:** `aws_lambda_runtime_python_version_check`

**CONFIDENCE:** MEDIUM

- The assessment lacks automation details, but the manual steps are generally correct. The potential for automation using AWS APIs is high, which increases confidence in an automated approach.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- **Re-classify:** The audit approach should be re-classified from "Manual" to "Automated." Implement an automated solution using boto3 or AWS CLI to check the Python runtime versions of Lambda functions against the supported list. This will ensure a more efficient and scalable compliance check process.
